mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the cross-sectional correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns is non-negative over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit expansion accelerates while industrial production stagnates, it indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples over the quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate exceeds 2σ of its 12-month mean while INDPRO growth falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth × INDPRO growth) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns in a multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, higher CPI readings signal that the Fed will delay cuts, extending the duration of tight liquidity and depressing forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, positive CPI surprises consistently correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns, or the forward return differential between high-CPI and low-CPI months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which directly supports near-term corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between monthly HOUST changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, or forward returns in months with positive housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the industrial production index below its 12-month moving average signals capacity utilization stress and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month rolling mean
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO breaches below its 12-month mean, or the predictive Rank IC for this threshold signal exceeds 0.08 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls grow rapidly but real consumer credit contracts, it indicates wage-driven inflation without broad demand support, prompting equity de-rating over the next month as margin sustainability declines.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong payroll growth and contracting credit fails to produce negative 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 40% of historical observations, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, accelerating real GDP growth directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth surprises fail to predict 5-day forward SPX returns, or the forward return spread between above-trend and below-trend GDP quarters is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
